Add the verification step to verify the certificate.

openssl verify -verbose -CAfile ca.cert test.cert

where test.cert is the generated certificate.
Use the tag "xenial" which is branched out.
Is this required ? you're changing the permission blindly on all the files under this directory.
Is this required ? you're changing the permission blindly on all the files in that directory.
Yes it is needed, First one is for the directory for SoftHSm operations with HwInfra. Second is for mount point, since it needs to be created before we mount the directory into the container.

TPM plugin is written by other developer and not me. It should be done otherwise it won't work. It can verified by building it on local test system. I would think twice before making such comments without testing and verifying.
It is a good practice to change permissions on only required files otherwise can lead to security issues, hence the comment.
I agree with Manjunath. What is the error you see if this step is omitted?
I know about the practice. I would ask the developer of TPM2-plugin to do the same if they are not aware of it. the modification is required, so it is not done "BLINDLY" as per your previous comment.
"text file busy" on bootstrap  and configure. and the make file target is asking for permission as well.
Did you try changing permissions only on bootstrap
You're the owner and committer of this patch and if something needs to be fixed/changed you have the right do so.

Yes, I did. it won't build without the permission on these 3 files
1. bootstrap
2. configure
3. Makefile.am
